feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: An EU of six, not 27, best way to 'reinforce Europe', Bruno Le Maire tells Euronews
entry.content: The former French finance minister argued that new impetus should be given to the European Union, namely by uniting six core countries to deliver on key issues in the face of foreign threats.

entry.title: Péter Magyar's EU summit debut signals Hungarian reset after change of guard
entry.content: Magyar was met with optimism at his first EU summit in Brussels, while Orbán rallied far-right allies nearby at much smaller venue urging him to veto the bloc's next seven-year budget. For the institutions, the Orbán era is over.

entry.title: Cuba's President Miguel Díaz-Canel admits 'urgent changes' needed to overcome crisis
entry.content: The oil blockade imposed by President Donald Trump in January has brought the island's already moribund economy to the brink of collapse, marked by power cuts sometimes lasting over 30 hours.

entry.title: Knicks revel in NBA victory parade as thousands of fans fill New York's streets
entry.content: New York erupted in jubilant scenes on Saturday when the Knicks broke a 53-year drought to defeat the San Antonio Spurs away in Texas.

entry.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
entry.content: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

entry.title: Iran's ayatollah says he approved US framework deal despite having 'different view'
entry.content: Meanwhile, US Vice President JD Vance defended the deal, saying he would likely head to Switzerland for talks to turn it into a long-term agreement.

entry.title: UK police say man under arrest after toddler 'ended up' in crocodile pen at zoo
entry.content: Cambridgeshire Constabulary said the toddler had sustained "serious injuries" and was in a "critical but stable" condition at Addenbrooke's Hospital in Cambridge.

entry.title: Foreign aid cuts and climate change pushing up migrant flows, IOM chief warns
entry.content: Several rich Western countries, particularly the United States but also many European nations, have cut their development aid budgets in recent years.

entry.title: Companies are rushing into AI, but adoption is still lagging, a KPMG executive says
entry.content: Companies may have AI strategies, but few can prove they are paying off, according to KPMG’s recent report. Euronews Next spoke with Mathieu Wallich-Petit, Head of Clients & Markets at KPMG France about how businesses can move from AI pilots to real impact.

entry.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
entry.content: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Lebanon: Tyre residents return to homes shattered by strikes
entry.content: Lebanese families returning to Tyre found their homes in ruins on 18 June after Israeli strikes and voiced cautious optimism about a new US-Iran agreement.

entry.title: French fishermen gather in Cherbourg as sector faces down multiple challenges
entry.content: In total, the French government said €13 million would be dedicated to the fishing sector for fuel consumption in April and May.

entry.title: Fact check: Can EU countries restrict or ban Israeli settlement imports?
entry.content: Several EU states are weighing measures against goods from Israeli settlements in the West Bank, East Jerusalem and the Golan Heights – all considered illegal under international law. Some argue that rules that exclude such goods from Israel’s tariff-free trade deal are not being properly enforced.

entry.title: Switzerland heading towards referendum on construction of new nuclear plants
entry.content: During the debates in parliament, Energy Minister Albert Rosti stressed the need to keep the nuclear power option open to guarantee the country's long-term energy supply.

entry.title: Bruce Springsteen, Stevie Wonder and Bono to attend Obama Centre's star-studded opening
entry.content: The $850 million campus, located on Chicago's South Side, includes a museum, library, sports facilities and community spaces.

entry.title: Keeping up appearances: Greece reveals Parthenon façade after 220 years
entry.content: For the first time in about 220 years, the west façade of the Parthenon in Greece can be see in its fullest possible form. External scaffolding has now been permanently removed from the monument, marking a key step in its long restoration.

entry.title: The energy transition's next test is security
entry.content: 'Countries that emerge strongest from the next phase of the transition will be those with diversified energy systems that remain secure, affordable and sustainable in a more uncertain world' writes Roberto Bocca from World Economic Forum’s Centre for Energy and Materials in an OpEd for Euronews.

entry.title: Germany and Uzbekistan deepen ties, President Steinmeier tells Euronews
entry.content: Trade between Germany and Uzbekistan exceeded $1.4 billion (€1.2bn) last year, while German investment reached $2 billion (€1.7bn). The two countries are also expanding educational exchanges, labour mobility programmes and business partnerships.

entry.title: New Lithuanian coalition formed as Social Democrats strike deal with partners
entry.content: Lithuania’s Social Democrats have formed a third coalition in just one and a half years, clearing the way for a new cabinet to be led by party leader Mindaugas Sinkevičius.

entry.title: How do Iranians and Israelis view the US-Iran deal?
entry.content: Residents in Tehran and Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions to the US-Iran agreement, with some welcoming an end to the fighting while others questioned whether the deal would hold.

entry.title: EU summit: Leaders and Zelenskyy to debate accession and peace negotiations
entry.content: Russia's war on Ukraine and unfair Chinese competition are expected to dominate the first day of the EU summit in Brussels.

entry.title: Chinese-British nationals jailed after being convicted of spying on exiled Hong Kong dissidents
entry.content: The sweeping National Security Law, which severely curtailed freedoms in Hong Kong, contributed to years of strained ties between Beijing and London.

entry.title: Palestine Football Association denies reported FIFA youth match against Israel
entry.content: The PFA said it had "not received any official communication or proposal from FIFA regarding such a match".

entry.title: Bulgarian prime minister opposes EU sanctions on Russia's Patriarch Kirill
entry.content: "The era of the Crusades is over," the Bulgarian prime minister said on Thursday as he confirmed his opposition to the proposal.

entry.title: Middle East flights update: Which airlines are returning to the region and when?
entry.content: While the US and Iran have signed a Memorandum of Understanding, European airlines are unlikely to restart flights until after the summer.

entry.title: Rutte tries to downplay Pete Hegseth's blistering attack on NATO
entry.content: As the Trump admininstration plans to pull a number troops out of Europe in the next six months, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth lambasted NATO defence ministers in Brussels, describing them as "shameful" and preoccupied with gender equality and climate change.

entry.title: Scotland for the win: Massachusetts becomes first US state to 'legalise' haggis
entry.content: The Scottish staple has been illegal in the US since the 1970s. Following a pre-World Cup campaign by one of Scotland's leading butchers and the efforts of a Scottish podcaster, Massachusetts has become the first US state to finally legalise haggis. Officially. Ish.

entry.title: Graft probe into Spanish ex-PM Zapatero expanded to daughters and secretary, court says
entry.content: The former premier defended his innocence during a three-hour hearing before Calama on Wednesday, becoming the first former or serving Spanish head of government to declare as a suspect in a corruption investigation.

entry.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
entry.content: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

entry.title: Albania's PM Edi Rama in Berlin: Europe today needs courage for enlargement
entry.content: Is Europe ready for its next Helmut Kohl moment? Albania's PM Edi Rama makes his case in Berlin

entry.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Toy Story 5' - You've got a friend in tech?
entry.content: The toys face a new existential crisis: technology. As timely as it is, can the fifth instalment recapture the magic lacking in 'Toy Story 4'?

entry.title: Poland arrests suspect in killing of Russian artist critical of Putin, PM Donald Tusk says
entry.content: Since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Russia has been accused of trying to assassinate its opponents abroad, including targeting exiled activists in France and Lithuania.

entry.title: Toronto celebrates as Ghana beat Panama with late World Cup goal
entry.content: Canada became the backdrop for emotional celebrations as Ghana secured a late World Cup victory over Panama in Toronto. Supporters from both nations turned the city into a vibrant meeting point before the decisive Group L match.

entry.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
entry.content: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

entry.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old 'proto-Stonehenge' just a few miles from the famous monument
entry.content: The structure consisted of two wooden posts that aligned precisely with the summer sunrise and winter sunset, suggesting ancient Britons were already tracking the Sun before the creation of Stonehenge.

entry.title: Salesforce France CEO: Both leaders and employees need to adapt to AI
entry.content: Speaking at Vivatech in Paris, Emilie Sidiqian, Salesforce France’s CEO, told Euronews Next how companies should embrace AI and why leaders must drive its adoption from the top.

entry.title: From Foxconn to Nvidia: Why France is so attractive for Europe’s AI infrastructure
entry.content: Foxconn, Nvidia and Mistral AI announce major AI infrastructure deals at Europe's VivaTech conference, with France's cheap nuclear energy and homegrown talent drawing global investment.

entry.title: EU-backed green bonds risk financing Chinese clean tech in third countries
entry.content: The EU has launched a €15-20bn green investment fund to boost sustainable projects, but a good share of the money is set to go towards buying Chinese clean technologies – including the same high-risk power inverters Brussels is trying to phase out.

entry.title: Joel and Ethan Coen named as this year’s Lumière Award recipients
entry.content: The 18th Lumière Awards will honour not one but two giants of the silver screen...

entry.title: England’s captain fantastic: Harry Kane praised for a ‘complete performance’ against Croatia
entry.content: The Bayern Munich striker scored twice as the Three Lions beat Croatia 4-2 in their World Cup group L opening fixture.

entry.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
entry.content: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

entry.title: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
entry.content: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

entry.title: World's longest non-stop flight set for 2027 take-off as Qantas confirms Sydney-London route
entry.content: Qantas’s long-awaited ‘Project Sunrise’ will see the world's longest non-stop passenger flight cover more than 16,000 kilometres between 19 and 22 hours.

entry.title: ‘If Ukraine burns, so will Moscow,’ Zelenskyy says after refinery fire
entry.content: The Ukrainian president said on Thursday that Kyiv will respond to all Russian attacks and that the morning strike on Moscow’s refinery — in response to Russia's attack on a UNESCO-protected cathedral earlier this week was “entirely justified.”

entry.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
entry.content: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Besides Trump, nobody believes deal will last, former US general says
entry.content: Retired US General Ben Hodges believes the situation is now "much worse" than four months ago. With Israel and Lebanon left out of the deal between the US and Iran, Hodges thinks the potential for Israel and Hezbollah to "resume fighting is really high".

entry.title: Inside the ultra-luxe Swiss mountainside retreat where Iran and the US are set to meet
entry.content: Initial negotiations between the US and Iran on the final agreement are set to take place at the Bürgenstock Resort Lake Lucerne this Friday.

entry.title: Voters head to the polls in key UK by-election that could determine Britain's next PM
entry.content: Burnham is regarded by many Labour MPs as the party's best chance of recovery following the disastrous May local elections.

entry.title: Misinformation targets Trump and European leaders at G7
entry.content: Social media users have been sharing old clips and taking statements by politicians out of context, in an apparent effort to highlight the strained relationship between the US and Europe.

entry.title: Cristiano Ronaldo faces media backlash as pundits slam Portugal's display against DR Congo
entry.content: The 41-year-old Portugal captain was hit by a barrage of criticism after a display that fell short of expectations at what is his sixth and, possibly, last World Cup.

entry.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
entry.content: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

entry.title: Shipping industry wary of resuming Strait of Hormuz navigation despite imminent deal
entry.content: The world's most important energy chokepoint may soon reopen, but insurance markets must regain confidence for the shipping sector to resume normal navigation. Meanwhile, Germany has dispatched two naval vessels for a possible mine clearance operation.

entry.title: Where is EV charging cheapest and fastest?
entry.content: Europe's EV charging network is growing fast, but prices and refuelling point density vary widely across the continent. Europe in Motion breaks down the data.

entry.title: VivaTech 2026: Tips from L'Oréal and PwC on personal vitality and business resilience
entry.content: From skincare giant L'Oréal to consulting firm PwC, business leaders at VivaTech 2026 had a shared message: personal vitality and organisational resilience are no longer a luxury — they are a competitive necessity.

entry.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
entry.content: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

entry.title: Hegseth says Europe must take the lead on building 'NATO 3.0'
entry.content: US president Donald Trump has repeatedly pushed for European NATO allies to divert more money to defence spending.

entry.title: 'The Ring' and 'Lilo & Stitch' actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
entry.content: Horror movie fans will recognize Chase from her performance as Samara Morgan, the main antagonist in American remake of 'The Ring'.

entry.title: Waldorf Astoria to open in London's iconic Admiralty Arch in autumn 2026 - in pictures
entry.content: Located on The Mall, the restored Grade I-listed landmark has been transformed into a luxury hotel with 114 rooms and suites, a spa, and two signature restaurants.

entry.title: Watch: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Versailles
entry.content: US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran at the Palace of Versailles on 18 June, marking a significant diplomatic step aimed at easing the Middle East conflict that has unsettled the region since February.

entry.title: Discover Lisbon like a local with our guide: Seven insider tips for its seven hills
entry.content: Venture off the beaten track and out of your comfort zone, dodge the crowds and, above all, steer clear of tourist traps. Here are seven ideas, one for each of Lisbon’s hills, devised by locals.

entry.title: Crimea will turn into an island, Ukraine’s defence minister says in a message to Russia
entry.content: Ukraine is methodically cutting off ground lines of communication for Russian forces into occupied Crimea, isolating the peninsula from Moscow’s forces and any supplies from Russia.

entry.title: UK: Tommy Robinson’s Oxford Union debate sparks protests and free speech backlash
entry.content: Tommy Robinson’s appearance at the Oxford Union prompts large protests and reignites the UK debate over free expression and platforms for extremists.

entry.title: Watch: Behind closed doors—Ukraine, taxes, and trade at this week's European Council Summit
entry.content: Tonight, Council President António Costa is gathering all twenty-seven national leaders for an exclusive, high-stakes working dinner. Sadly, he forgot to invite your reporter, but that doesn't stop us from seeing what will be served on the multi-course menu.

entry.title: Newsletter: Zelenskyy in town for talks with NATO and EU leaders
entry.content: Also in this newsletter: Former US commander general Ben Hodges reacts to the signing of the US-Iran peace deal, and former French Economy Minister Bruno Le Maire tells Euronews the EU needs to function as a core group of six.

entry.title: Ukraine strikes Moscow oil refinery for second time this week
entry.content: It comes as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte in Brussels.

entry.title: Europe Today: NATO, Ukraine and the battle over Europe's future budget
entry.content: NATO defence ministers are gathering to prepare for next month’s leaders’ summit in Ankara. Among those attending is Pentagon chief and US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

entry.title: The world’s best skylines have just been named – and none of the top 10 are in Europe
entry.content: Asia dominated the list, with eight spots making the top 10 across China, Malaysia and Japan.

entry.title: Synthetic opioids: can Europe keep up with a new drug created every week?
entry.content: Fifty new drugs were detected across Europe in 2025 alone, many of them highly potent synthetic opioids. Can the EU’s drugs strategy keep pace with an increasingly dangerous and fast-moving drug landscape?

entry.title: EU leaders meet in Brussels to discuss Ukraine, China shock and €2 trillion budget
entry.content: Russia's war on Ukraine, unfair Chinese competition and the next long-term budget top the agenda of the EU summit.

entry.title: Hidden figures: Stephan Gladieu on photographing the people of North Korea
entry.content: Ahead of his exhibition at the Musée des Confluences in Lyon, French photographer Stephan Gladieu talked to European Lens about his series titled ‘North Korea’, a collection of photographs taken over three years and several trips to the reclusive country.

entry.title: These luxury European hotels have been included in a new hall of fame list
entry.content: From Paris and Monte Carlo to Lake Como and St Moritz, nine of Europe's most iconic luxury hotels have earned places in Esquire's Hotel Hall of Fame.

entry.title: European lawsuits over child suicide and self-harm caused by social media pile up
entry.content: Cases are being heard before courts in Italy and France, while the family of a Scottish teen is part of a lawsuit in the United States.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Ronaldo fails to score in disappointing Portugal opener as England dismantle Croatia in 4-2 win
entry.content: Ronaldo, who matches Messi in a record sixth World Cup appearance, failed to become the first player to score in six different tournaments in his nation’s opening fixture against DR Congo.

entry.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran deal at Palace of Versailles
entry.content: Video released by the French presidency shows Donald Trump signing an agreement aimed at ending the war with Iran during a dinner at Versailles, with French President Emmanuel Macron seated beside him.

entry.title: Trump signs initial deal to end Iran war and open Strait of Hormuz as nuclear talks continue
entry.content: Trump signed an initial framework deal to restore the pre-war status quo between Iran and the United States, inking an initial agreement towards ending a war he started with Israel on 28 February.

entry.title: Trump welcomed to Versailles for dinner with Macron
entry.content: Donald Trump was welcomed to the Palace of Versailles on Wednesday evening for a private reception and dinner hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron following the conclusion of the G7 summit in Evian.

entry.title: AI dominates conversations at VivaTech
entry.content: VivaTech's 10th edition draws 200,000 visitors to Paris, with Jeff Bezos and Emmanuel Macron among the guests. AI dominates conversations on the show floor. Euronews finds a personal survival capsule for when the robots take over.

entry.title: Row between Elon Musk and German broadcaster ZDF sparks major controversy
entry.content: German broadcaster ZDF has reacted to a cease-and-desist suit from Elon Musk, deleting a line from a programme intro over his links to UK right-wing activist Tommy Robinson.

entry.title: WFP welcomes €695m donation from US as it warns acute hunger to worsen in 13 hotspots
entry.content: The WFP has said it wants to reach 110 million people in the most acute need around the world this year, for which it would require €11 billion.

entry.title: Finland's parliament passes bill in support of lifting total ban on nuclear weapons
entry.content: Finland dropped decades of military non-alliance to join NATO in April 2023 in response to Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

entry.title: Europeans leave G7 feeling relieved, balancing self-interest and flattery to keep Trump in check
entry.content: Macron hailed the G7 as an "objectively successful" summit after leaders agreed on key issues from Ukraine and Iran to critical minerals. European officials point to growing convergence with Washington, but Trump's volatile diplomacy continues to cloud transatlantic relations.

entry.title: Six takeaways from the G7 summit in Évian
entry.content: G7 leaders gathered in France for a high-stakes meeting over the last three days to discuss Ukraine, the Middle East and world challenges. Here is what you need to know.

entry.title: How can Europe compete with the US and China economically? MEPs debate on The Ring
entry.content: From Artificial Intelligence to rare minerals, from Trump's tariffs to China's cheap exports - in this edition MEPs Jörgen Walborn (EPP) and Lina Gálvez (S&D) talk about the global economic challenges that lie ahead and that impact all of us in Europe.

entry.title: Zelenskyy heads to Brussels buoyed by new G7 commitments
entry.content: Ukraine secured fresh G7 military and diplomatic backing, including air defence support, as pressure grows on Russia to negotiate.

entry.title: Germany and Poland sign new defence deal as balance of power in Europe shifts
entry.content: Poland’s importance as a logistics hub for Ukraine, alongside its growing economy and heavy defence investment, has made it a compelling partner for Germany and other core European countries.

entry.title: Luigi Mangione to use psychiatric defence in health CEO murder trial, judge says
entry.content: Brian Thompson was killed in December 2024 as he walked to a Manhattan hotel for UnitedHealth Group’s annual investor conference.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 17th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 17th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon ahead of anticipated US-Iran peace deal
entry.content: While violence has declined in Lebanon since a US-Iran agreement to end the Middle East war was announced on Monday, Israeli strikes on the south have killed at least five people since the deal, according to Lebanese state media.

entry.title: Bus with Belarus children's football team hit by a drone in Russia, Moscow claims
entry.content: A bus carrying a children's football team from Belarus in Russia's border Bryansk region was hit by a drone, according to Russian officials. Moscow claims it was attacked by Ukraine, but Kyiv denies it carried out any operations in the area.

entry.title: Rutte heads to Washington to smooth NATO tensions ahead of July summit
entry.content: EXCLUSIVE: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte is travelling to Washington next Tuesday ahead of the annual NATO summit in Ankara in July. Relations between the US and its NATO allies have been strained after Trump accused European nations of abandoning the US during its war in Iran.

entry.title: UK minister ‘very confident’ of getting triple EU reset deal done at July summit
entry.content: Nick Thomas-Symonds tells Euronews he’s “very confident” London and Brussels will close new pacts on agri-food exports, electricity and youth mobility when they convene for a summit in July, as domestic pressure mounts on his government.

entry.title: From regime change to regime deal? How Tehran's political camps are reacting to leaked US-Iran deal
entry.content: Iran's hardliners have framed the framework deal as a victory, even as Trump dismissed reports of a $300bn reconstruction fund and warned he would resume strikes if Tehran did not comply.

entry.title: Read the full text of the leaked 14-point US-Iran draft agreement
entry.content: It remains unclear whether the 14-point draft that was circulated at G7 meetings in France will be the version formalised at a ceremony in Switzerland on Friday.

entry.title: Albania's PM Edi Rama: Is Europe ready for its next Helmut Kohl moment?
entry.content: In Berlin, once the symbol of a divided Germany and Europe, Albania’s Prime Minister Edi Rama delivered a speech on Europe's future. Rama called on Europe to seize a “Helmut Kohl moment”. Euronews reports on the speech, delivered at the German Committee on Eastern European Economic Relations.

entry.title: Paris just opened a floating bookshop under Notre-Dame - and it has DJ sets
entry.content: The floating venue, called Nanna, offers more than 5,000 books in a self-service setup where visitors can borrow freely - with no obligation to return them.

entry.title: Norwegian Crown Princess undergoes successful lung transplant
entry.content: The 52-year-old will remain in hospital for a number of weeks so that doctors can monitor for any complications, Arnt Fiane, a professor of medicine at the University of Oslo, said.

entry.title: Afghan government staff told to abandon smartphones or face prison sentence
entry.content: How widely the rule is being implemented across Afghanistan and the scale of its impact were not entirely clear.

entry.title: Netherlands hosts Japan’s emperor Naruhito to boost 400-year ties
entry.content: The Netherlands welcomed Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako to Amsterdam on 17 June during an official visit aimed at celebrating the long-standing relationship between the two countries.

entry.title: Groundbreaking US astronaut Christina Koch wins top Spanish award
entry.content: Koch was part of the four-member crew of the Artemis II mission which in April flew around the Moon and is widely viewed as a potential candidate for future lunar-surface missions.

entry.title: Israeli settlers torch mosque in West Bank village of Jiljilya
entry.content: Residents of Jiljilya, north of Ramallah, awoke on 17 June to find their mosque set alight overnight, with burnt tyres, Hebrew graffiti reading "revenge" and extensive damage left behind in an area of the occupied West Bank where Israelis are prohibited from entering.

entry.title: Trump jokes ‘I’m the boss’ as he arrives for G7 meeting
entry.content: Donald Trump joked that he was “the boss” as he arrived for a G7 meeting in France on Wednesday. The light-hearted remark came before leaders gathered for talks on trade, economic challenges and global financial stability alongside representatives from partner nations.

entry.title: Iran pledges no nuclear weapons as US commits $300bn, leaked deal draft shows
entry.content: The full US-Iran framework deal to end the war, as circulated during the G7 summit, would see Iran abandon its pursuit of a nuclear weapon in exchange for financial incentives to comply with the agreement.

entry.title: This hotel chain just launched an ‘anti-appy hour’ to get guests off their phones
entry.content: Guests at YOTEL hotels will be able to block their access to social media apps on their phone using a Bloom card.

entry.title: An influencer, a PR specialist and a bishop: Who are the latest EU-sanctioned disinformation actors?
entry.content: The Council of the European Union added a further 10 individuals and one entity to its sanctions list on 15 June, accusing them of conducting hybrid manipulation and foreign interference activities on behalf of Russia.

entry.title: Glue, glorious glue: Sticking together the Sagrada Familia’s central towers
entry.content: Did you know that Loctite technology bonds were crucial in sticking stone and steel together in the Sagrada Familia temple’s 826 panels? At 172 metres tall, it is the world’s highest temple and was last week blessed by Pope Leo XVI.

entry.title: England out to avenge 2018 semifinal loss to Croatia as they start World Cup campaign
entry.content: England coach Thomas Tuchel said he expected former Real Madrid legend Luka Modrić and Manchester City's Mateo Kovačić to still be the "heartbeat" of the Croatian side.

entry.title: 'An unexpected story of friendship': Sean Penn to direct police officer drama on January 6 riots
entry.content: Sean Penn has signed a deal with Warner Bros. to direct a film he's scripted about the deadly attack on the US Capitol on January 6 in 2021.

entry.title: Jeff Bezos at VivaTech: We need to colonise the Moon to save Earth
entry.content: The Amazon founder told VivaTech in Paris that moving heavy industry off Earth is the only way to reconcile economic growth with a liveable planet — and the moon is where it starts.

entry.title: EU Parliament approves 'strictest-ever' migration law
entry.content: The European Parliament gave the final green light to a new EU law allowing controversial return hubs outside the bloc and home searches, as conservatives voted with far-right groups to pass the legislation.

entry.title: Four Seasons has just opened a luxury wellness resort on the Red Sea
entry.content: Guests can book in for a wellness journey that includes sunrise yoga on the beach, sauna and cold plunge sessions and healthy plates throughout the day.

entry.title: Messi hat-trick fuels Argentina's World Cup title hopes
entry.content: Fans in Buenos Aires celebrated as Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick to lead defending champions Argentina to a 3-0 victory over Algeria in their World Cup opener.

entry.title: Portugal urges EU rethink on carbon market cuts for industry
entry.content: Portugal has urged Brussels to pause cuts to free carbon allowances, warning they could damage competitiveness and slow industrial decarbonisation.

entry.title: Hundreds of cats rescued from being eaten in Vietnam
entry.content: More than 500 cats were seized in Vietnam after police dismantled a cat meat trafficking network, with dozens of animals later reunited with their owners.

entry.title: More people get news from social media than traditional outlets, study shows
entry.content: Fifty-four percent of people said they used social media as a news source at least once a week compared to the 51% using traditional media, like TV, radio or news websites.

entry.title: ‘We have always been friends’: Trump and Meloni make amends at G7
entry.content: Trump and Meloni made amends at the G7 in Evian, joking about their recent spat over the Iran war. "We have always been friends," Meloni said.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 17th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 17th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Thousands flock to Art Basel in Switzerland as market outlook improves
entry.content: Art Basel 2025 in Basel draws thousands as high-end works by Picasso, Warhol and Hockney fuel a recovering global art market after years of decline.

entry.title: New fleet of electric hydrofoil boats to be deployed in the Maldives
entry.content: The Maldives will introduce up to 100 electric hydrofoil boats, promising smoother and more sustainable transfers between its islands.

entry.title: Putin meets with ASEAN leaders in Russia as G7 vow 'unwavering' support for Ukraine
entry.content: It comes after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with G7 leaders in France. Zelenskyy said on Wednesday they had agreed on the "additional strengthening of Ukraine’s air defense" and new measures against Moscow.

entry.title: France and Germany call for European AI sovereignty at VivaTech
entry.content: When the US suspended access to Anthropic's latest AI models last week, it reminded Europe that rules can change overnight. At VivaTech in Paris, France and Germany said the answer is sovereignty — and the window to build it is now.

entry.title: Europeans head to World Cup in droves, but which country has seen the best supporter turnout?
entry.content: As the group stage heats up across Mexico, Canada and the US, Europe in Motion takes a look at the most committed supporters at the World Cup.

entry.title: All of Uzbekistan readies to watch history at its first World Cup
entry.content: Schools, offices and city squares across Uzbekistan are setting up screens for Thursday's 7 am kick-off against Colombia, as the country makes its World Cup debut under coach Fabio Cannavaro. "In the heart of every Uzbek, this is a matter of pride and honour," an education ministry official said.

entry.title: Javier Bardem joins Hollywood greats at landmark ceremony
entry.content: Spanish film star Javier Bardem was honoured in Los Angeles on 16 June with a place in the celebrated forecourt of the TCL Chinese Theatre, a distinction reserved for some of cinema's biggest names.

entry.title: Will Cristiano Ronaldo finally win the World Cup?
entry.content: Ronaldo, now 41, is gearing up for what's set to be his final World Cup appearance. And it could be Portugal's best ever shot at lifting the trophy.

entry.title: Watch: Cash, power, and politics—inside Gianni Infantino’s World Cup empire
entry.content: A massive revolt is brewing for football’s most powerful man. For many, it would be a career game changer. But will it be for Infantino?

entry.title: Zapatero appears before National Court as first former PM charged in Spain's democracy
entry.content: The former president will testify on Wednesday and Thursday over the Plus Ultra bailout and the jewels found in his Ferraz office, valued at more than one million euros.

entry.title: Ukraine: Russian drone attack on Zaporizhzhia home kills one, injures seven
entry.content: At least one person was killed and seven others injured after a Russian drone struck a residential building in the southeastern Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia on 16 June, sparking a major fire and causing extensive damage, Ukrainian officials said.

entry.title: Germany reportedly prepping to take part in Strait of Hormuz safeguarding mission
entry.content: The German defence ministry has said naval units are already on standby for a possible deployment.

entry.title: Newsletter: G7 aligns on Ukraine in rare moment of unity
entry.content: Also in this newsletter: What to expect as G7 leaders meet top AI and tech executives.

entry.title: G7 leaders agree to increase weapons deliveries for Ukraine, step up pressure on Russian economy
entry.content: G7 leaders pledge more weapons for Ukraine, including air defence and long-range systems, and signal readiness to tighten sanctions on Russia's oil and gas sector.

entry.title: One killed in Russian drone strike on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia
entry.content: Ukraine's Air Force said Russia attacked Ukraine with 119 drones overnight, of which 97 were intercepted. 20 drones were reported hitting 11 locations across Ukraine.

entry.title: Washington's Lincoln Memorial pool turns green days after renovation
entry.content: An algae bloom has turned Washington's newly renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool green just days after a $14 million makeover.

entry.title: Iranian oil tankers pass US blockade ahead of peace deal signing ceremony
entry.content: The naval blockade began on 13 April and targeted all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas.

entry.title: G7 leaders press Trump on Ukraine as summit wraps up
entry.content: The eyes of the world are on Evian-les-Bains, where G7 leaders are gathering for their third day and final day of talks. Beyond the photo ops, European leaders used this precious face time with the US President to urge him to break the deadlock on Ukraine.

entry.title: ‘Coolcations’ in Europe: New index reveals the best places to escape the summer heat
entry.content: A new summer heat escape index has ranked Europe's coolest destinations for summer travel in 2026, with Iceland, Finland and Norway leading the way.

entry.title: G7 summit: Leaders turn to economy as China Shock 2.0 looms
entry.content: The third and final day of the G7 summit in Évian, France, is underway. This morning's discussion is dedicated to the economic imbalances, as the China Shock 2.0 looms large over the largest Western economies.

entry.title: AI takes centre stage at G7 as Western fears over US ‘kill switch’ get real
entry.content: Western allies arrived at the G7 AI summit unsettled after the Trump administration cut off foreign access to Anthropic's most powerful models, confirming Europe's kill-switch fears and providing yet another boost for its tech sovereignty agenda.

entry.title: How is the EU tackling drug trafficking and synthetic substance use?
entry.content: Europe's new drugs strategy runs to 2030, but critics warn it prioritises policing over public health. Watch the video!

entry.title: Hands, feet and nose: Javier Bardem makes his mark in Hollywood and honours his family's legacy
entry.content: Javier Bardem was centre stage at a moving ceremony in Los Angeles as he paid tribute to his mother Pilar Bardem on Hollywood's Walk of Fame. But the Spanish actor departed from custom by pressing his nose into the wet cement along with his hand and footprints,

entry.title: From Jeff Bezos to Europe’s AI push: Everything to know about VivaTech 2026
entry.content: Euronews will be at the tech conference, which is now in its 10th edition.

entry.title: Direct trains from Bucharest to Istanbul, Sofia and Varna have restarted until October
entry.content: Romania's national railway has relaunched daily direct summer trains from Bucharest to destinations in Türkiye and Bulgaria, with fares starting from €27.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 17th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 17th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Patriot, IRIS-T, Flamingo: Germany's long-range weapons gap
entry.content: Global shortages of Patriot missiles are putting air defence systems under pressure. Germany's IRIS-T offers one answer, but Berlin still lacks the long-range firepower needed for deep strikes.

entry.title: Germany and Poland set to boost military cooperation and shared duties in new defence deal
entry.content: Berlin and Warsaw are set to expand their defence collaborations in a new deal aiming to boost plans to protect the Baltic Sea and enhance military cooperation and communications between the two European allies.

entry.title: Kazakhstan’s skies welcome Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup
entry.content: Around 150 pilots from 33 countries have gathered in Kazakhstan's Zhetysu region for Central Asia's first-ever Paragliding World Cup.

entry.title: Lionel Messi ties Miroslav Klose for most World Cup goals as Argentina cruise past Algeria 3-0
entry.content: France finds a way to break a goalless first half as they cruise past Senegal, the African champions whose recent AFCON title was stripped on a technicality and awarded to Morocco, delivering a 3-1 win campaign opener.

entry.title: FBI foils plot targeting White House UFC event, five arrested across multiple states
entry.content: Five suspects from Ohio, Missouri and California are in custody after the FBI learned of the threat four days before the South Lawn event.

entry.title: UN chief visits Haiti as gang violence soars and number of displaced hits 1.5 million
entry.content: Guterres’ first stop was the headquarters of the new gang-suppression force, which the UN Security Council approved in September.

entry.title: Banned but still paid: How disinformation accounts keep monetising on Facebook
entry.content: A new study shows that Facebook pages flagged more than 10 times for fake content by fact-checkers still make money off their content, including after suspension.

entry.title: At least 58 states and territories contaminated by landmines, UN human rights chief says
entry.content: At least 945 people were killed and 4,325 injured by landmines and explosive remnants of war in 2024 alone, the UN said, citing the Landmine and Cluster Munition Monitor.

entry.title: US support could be Ukraine's 'trump card' against Russia, says Ukrainian former Prime Minister
entry.content: Former Ukrainian Prime Minister Arseniy Yatsenyuk told Euronews that Moscow had expected a “spat” between G7 leaders and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, but said the “optics of the summit are much better than expected.” He added that the key question now is whether Ukraine and the EU can win Donald Trump’s sup

entry.title: Church of Sweden launches inquiry into PM's wife over unspecified ethical concerns
entry.content: The Lutheran Church of Sweden, which has some 5.4 million members in a country of 10.6 million, formally separated from the Swedish state in 2000.

entry.title: UK lions predict England's World Cup fate
entry.content: Lionesses and lions at a UK sanctuary were given World Cup-themed boxes to choose from, with keepers treating their picks as playful predictions for England's group-stage matches.

entry.title: Trump ballroom cost soars to €516m with half coming from taxpayers, US media say
entry.content: The project began last year when Trump, with little warning and without consulting Congress, tore down the entire historic East Wing of the White House.

entry.title: EU Parliament rejects member states’ first draft of long-term budget
entry.content: EU lawmakers called the document “insufficient” and criticised proposed spending cuts of €32.8 billion. Because the budget requires Parliament’s approval, pressure is mounting to reach an agreement by the end of 2026.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 16th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 16th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: UK 'investigating' after yacht says Russian warship fired 'warning shots' in Channel
entry.content: The incident comes two days after armed British forces boarded and detained a sanctioned tanker in the Channel that is suspected of being part of Russia’s shadow fleet.

entry.title: Norway's Crown Prince Haakon cuts workload to be with gravely ill wife, palace says
entry.content: Mette-Marit, 52, was diagnosed in 2018 with a rare form of pulmonary fibrosis, or scarring of the lungs, that causes breathing difficulties.

entry.title: Lídia Jorge honoured with Austrian State Prize for European Literature
entry.content: It is a prize “awarded since 1965 for the literary work of a European author whose writing has gained international renown”, reads a note on Austria’s culture ministry website.

entry.title: Lukashenka apologises to Zelenskyy, calls him 'young and inexperienced'
entry.content: Belarus leader Aliaksandr Lukashenka said that Belarus poses no threat to Ukraine as he apologised to Volodymyr Zelenskyy for his harsh comments in the past, while saying Ukraine’s president was “not a military man”.

entry.title: Iran coach calls team World Cup's 'most oppressed' after rushed departure from US
entry.content: Amir Ghalenoei said his side was ordered to fly back to Tijuana hours after Monday's 2-2 draw with New Zealand, while captain Mehdi Taremi called it "a disaster for us," adding that the team's "goal and wish is to pursue peace and happiness".

entry.title: Qatar 'cautiously optimistic' on US-Iran framework deal before Friday's signing
entry.content: Qatar said the framework agreement to be signed on Friday was a first step rather than a final settlement, adding that it would take time to return to "business as usual" even once the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Trump separately praised Qatar's mediating role and its "great bravery".

entry.title: Swedish court imprisons man convicted of pressuring his wife to sell sexual services
entry.content: Prosecutor Ida Annerstedt told the AFP news agency at the start of the trial in April that the woman had "a serious fear" of her husband.

entry.title: New report raises concerns over Russian propaganda spread by Europe’s flagship AI company Mistral
entry.content: All four Mistral models evaluated by the Institute of the Estonian Language were deemed likely to use Russian propaganda in their answers.

entry.title: Russian artist and Putin critic Semyon Skrepetsky shot dead in Poland, officials say
entry.content: Skrepetsky was known for his sometimes provocative caricatures, which targeted prominent Russian political figures ranging from Putin and Soviet leader Joseph Stalin.

entry.title: European Parliament lifts immunity of Italian MEP embroiled in Huawei corruption scandal
entry.content: The European Parliament has voted to lift the immunity of Italian MEP Fulvio Martusciello, who is under investigation in an alleged scandal of corruption and illicit lobbying practices by tech company Huawei.

entry.title: Kyiv seeks license to produce Patriot systems for Ukraine and Europe
entry.content: With Russia deliberately targeting Ukraine’s energy system each winter, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said at the G7 summit that he wants to bring the war to an end before the next cold season. Kyiv now holds a stronger position both on the battlefield and in its strikes deep in Russia.

entry.title: Beyond 'Disclosure Day': Our picks of Steven Spielberg’s most underrated movies
entry.content: Steven Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' has a strong box office opening despite mixed reviews. Felt alienated by it or looking to revisit / discover some of the director's filmography? The Euronews Culture team shares their picks of Spielberg’s underrated gems.

entry.title: UN says 2.4m refugees will need resettling in 2027 and warns of shortage of options
entry.content: Afghan refugees are the largest group in need of resettlement, followed by people from South Sudan, Sudan, Syria and Rohingya refugees from Myanmar, living in vast camps in Bangladesh.

entry.title: Sinkevičius says he will lead reshuffled Lithuanian government following coalition turmoil
entry.content: The three-party coalition must still sign an agreement formalising the new alliance, expected to take place on 18 June, which will then be approved by the country's president.

entry.title: Who is Pico Lopes, the unlikely Cape Verde World Cup hero recruited through LinkedIn?
entry.content: The national team's call came from 4,500 km away across the Ocean and not in the way most people would expect.

entry.title: London West End's Shaftesbury Theatre to be renamed to honour Judi Dench
entry.content: The name change of one of the West End’s most iconic stages celebrates one of the greatest British performers. Dame Judi Dench has been left “truly overwhelmed” by the news.

entry.title: Russian Tu-22M3 bomber crashes during training flight
entry.content: Russian Tu-22M3 strategic bomber has crashed in the Irkutsk region during a training flight, amid reports of engine failure. The Defence Ministry said it was not carrying weapons and the crew ejected safely. Four people were taken to hospital.

entry.title: Trump tells Netanyahu 'be responsible with Lebanon, let Syria handle Hezbollah' at G7
entry.content: Trump questions Netanyahu's methods, says Beirut strike was excessive, and points to Syria’s President as the most “capable” of countering Hezbollah.

entry.title: Watch: German chancellor surprises Trump with 'Trump 47' football jersey at G7
entry.content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz presented U.S. President Donald Trump with a personalized football jersey during the G7 summit in France.

entry.title: MEPs approve EU-US trade deal despite Trump’s new trade war threats
entry.content: The agreement removes EU tariffs on US goods while accepting a 15 percent levy in the other direction.

entry.title: Education: Where in the EU has the most early school dropouts?
entry.content: The number of young Europeans leaving education early is falling, but geography and employment prospects still shape the picture. How do EU countries compare with each other?

entry.title: Humanoid robots join models at fashion show in Seoul
entry.content: Humanoid robots wearing designer outfits shared the runway with human models at a futuristic fashion show in Seoul, highlighting advances in robotics and AI.

entry.title: Moscow oil refinery ablaze after retaliatory Ukrainian drone strike
entry.content: Kyiv claimed the attack as retaliation for Russia's intensifying aerial campaign, a day after drones damaged a UNESCO-listed monastery complex in the capital.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 16th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 16th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Anish Kapoor brings darkness, mirrors and the 'sublime' to London’s Hayward Gallery
entry.content: A major new exhibition showcasing the work of Turner Prize-winner Anish Kapoor has opened at London’s Hayward Gallery, bringing together blood-red suspended sculptures, distorted mirror installations and void-like forms that warp perception and space.

entry.title: Ships start to trickle through Strait of Hormuz, but who will run it still in doubt
entry.content: Trump said the strait would be "completely open" by Friday, but key details over who will manage the crucial waterway remain unresolved. Meanwhile, Iran has made further claims that it will control Hormuz in the future based on its interpretation of the framework deal with the US.

entry.title: Rare first edition of ‘Wuthering Heights’ (complete with spelling mistakes) to go up for auction
entry.content: It’s the first copy of the Gothic novel in the publisher’s original cloth binding to be auctioned since 1908.

entry.title: Europe’s most welcoming countries and cities for expats
entry.content: A new report looks at factors including foreign-born employment rates, safety, and overall expat experience.

entry.title: Hundreds of cats destined for slaughter rescued in Vietnam as police dismantle theft ring
entry.content: Vietnamese police arrested nine people and rescued more than 400 live cats destined for slaughter, reuniting dozens with their owners. Around 100 of the animals later died from their ordeal.

entry.title: The best FIFA World Cup 2026 aircraft liveries
entry.content: National carriers have sent their teams to the world’s biggest stage in style aboard special themed planes.

entry.title: Seaplanes: Greece-Albania link takes off, with more international routes planned
entry.content: Hellenic Seaplanes is expanding its network, introducing new routes that will connect the city of Ioannina with Vlorë and Pogradec in neighbouring Albania.

entry.title: Fact check: Is the EU fining member states that don't accept migrants?
entry.content: A major reform of the EU’s migration and asylum system has recently entered into force, prompting political figures in France to criticise one of its key provisions. However, their claims are misleading.

entry.title: Europe's top listening hotels: Check in for music, DJs and vinyl
entry.content: From vinyl-fuelled cocktail lounges in Athens and London to a luxury listening suite in Lake Como, European hotels are turning sound into the main attraction, blending analogue music, DJ culture and design-led hospitality into immersive stays.

entry.title: Crowds flock to JR's giant cave installation on Paris' Pont Neuf
entry.content: Visitors queued to explore French artist JR's new immersive installation, which transforms Paris' oldest bridge, the Pont Neuf, into a giant cave open around the clock.

entry.title: A cave for a crowd: French artist JR opens 'Caverne' art work on Pont Neuf bridge in Paris
entry.content: Open until 28 June, JR’s ephemeral art installation, by the master of XXL photography, immerses Parisian passers-by in a vast cave.

entry.title: WATCH: Bolton says Trump played like violin by Iran
entry.content: Iran outmanoeuvred US President Donald Trump "like a violin" in negotiations, walking away with far better terms after sensing his desperation for a deal to end the war, former National Security Adviser John Bolton told Euronews.

entry.title: €31bn drug trade, 7,600 deaths: How the EU plans to tackle the drug crisis
entry.content: 29 million Europeans use drugs every year, and the market has an estimated €31 billion value. The European Council has released a new framework to completely change how the EU handles this enormous problem, from enforcement to public health.

entry.title: Protesting and cheering as Iran's World Cup match divides diaspora
entry.content: Iran's opening World Cup match drew both supporters and protesters outside the stadium near Los Angeles, highlighting divisions within the Iranian-American community.

entry.title: Eight killed in US Air Force B-52 bomber crash in California
entry.content: Eight people were killed when a B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in Southern California. Officials said the aircraft was on a routine test mission and the cause remains under investigation.

entry.title: Iran 'played Trump like a violin' in deal, former national security adviser Bolton tells Euronews
entry.content: Bolton tells Euronews the US sacrificed its strategic leverage as Trump prioritised lowering oil prices, while Iran walks away with the deal it wanted.

entry.title: G7 summit: Trump asks Netanyahu to be 'more responsible' in Lebanon
entry.content: The second day of the G7 summit in Évian, France, is underway. The topics on the agenda include Russia's war on Ukraine, the situation in the Middle East and international partnerships.
